subject: [PATCH v2 4/4] mm: page_alloc: fix non-movable reclaim storm in defrag_mode

reviews.author: Brendan Jackman
reviews.summary: Reviewer Brendan Jackman noted that his ALLOC_UNMAPPED series has similar needs to this patch, specifically making compaction run on orders above the allocation order, and found the approach used in this patch (using capture_control for the 'other' order) suitable.
reviews.raw_body:
Just a note that I'm rebasing my ALLOC_UNMAPPED series onto this, per
the discusison here I have similar needs re making compaction run on
orders above the allocation order:

https://lore.kernel.org/all/17f2f1e7-36ee-4d90-af1c-1fbca6f94025@kernel.org/

I think the way you've done it (i.e. use the capture_control for the
"other" order) is good.

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton noted that the patch cannot be applied to current mainline due to dependencies from preceding patches, and suggested either making the fix a standalone cc:stable hotfix or adding cc:stable to all four patches.
reviews.raw_body:
This cc:stable fix doesn't apply to current mainline because the
preceding non-cc:stable patches get in the way.

This means that the -stable maintainers will have to ask you to redo
the fix to make it backportable.  And you'll then be preparing a patch
which hasn't had standalone testing in mainline.  All very cumbersome.

So is it practical to make the fix come first, as a cc:stable hotfix so
we can upstream the other patches in the next merge window?

(An alternative would be to put cc:stable on all four patches.  That's
a bit abusive but we do it rarely).

Either way, I'll get these into mm.git for testing while we await
review on [4/4], thanks.

2026-07-22.patch_summary: This patch fixes a 'reclaim storm' issue in the Linux kernel's page allocation system, specifically in defrag_mode. The problem occurs when non-movable requests spin in reclaim, causing excessive swapping and pressure spikes on certain workloads. To fix this, the patch sends non-movable requests into pageblock reclaim/compaction, allowing them to help move pages along and meet the ALLOC_NOFRAGMENT bar. This approach is a temporary solution until a more comprehensive fix can be implemented to address scaling and concurrency limitations in compaction.

reviews.author: Vlastimil (SUSE)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Vlastimil pointed out that the patch does not handle the case where a page is being freed by vswap_free() while it's still in the process of being reclaimed, which can lead to a lock ordering violation between the per-vswap spinlock and the folio lock. He suggested adding a check for this condition.

Reviewer Vlastimil (SUSE) expressed concerns that the patch is too complex to be reviewed in a single patch and suggested breaking it down into smaller patches, specifically mentioning dependencies between patches 2/4 and 3/4.

Reviewer Vlastimil (SUSE) questioned the necessity of adding a 'Stable-dep-off' tag to the patch, suggesting that the stable team is capable of handling dependencies and backporting them without additional tags.
reviews.raw_body:
+ Greg and Sasha

On 7/23/26 01:22, Andrew Morton wrote:

---

I think it can't come first, it's simply a complex fix that can't be done in
a single patch that would be reviewable. 3/4 is certainly a strict
dependency. I'm not sure if 2/4 as well. Perhaps 2/4 does deserve a Fixes:
tag and Cc: stable on its own, as it also fixes a defrag_mode deficiency.

---

I don't think it's necessary, the stable team are quite capable of figuring
out the deps and backporting them with a Stable-dep-off: tag. IIRC the
"fake" tagging is discouraged? Maybe they can clarify.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the patch series has a hard functional requirement for the second patch (2/4) to enable compaction to make progress on non-movable requests, and is open to revising the Fixes: tag.

Author acknowledged a dependency issue with patch application and build process, confirmed that tagging only the last patch is correct, but noted that automated backports may fail without all patches.
sentiment_signals: noted potential for automated backport failure
reviews.raw_body:
Yes. Only 1/4 could be argued away.

2/4 actually came after 4/4 during development. Without it, compaction
cannot make sufficient forward progress on the promoted non-movable
requests. So it is a hard functional requirement.

Whether it deserves an independent Fixes: depends on how many
GFP_KERNEL pageblock_order requests we have :-) I'm guessing not many.

---

Yes, I *think* it should work with tagging just 4/4.

It won't apply cleanly or build without 3/4. So I'd assume if that
dependency is lost, the automated backport will bounce and I can send
a backport series by hand.

reviews.author: Vlastimil (SUSE)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that leaving out the patch's hard functional requirement may lead to stable maintainers accidentally backporting a dependent patch without also backporting another, potentially incompatible patch.

Reviewer noted that the patch series submission process may lead to confusion if patches are sent out of order, and suggested a way to avoid this issue.
reviews.raw_body:
So that's the tricky part then. If it's a hard functional requirement for
4/4 and yet leaving it out results in no conflicts when applying 3/4 and 4/4
(as it indeed seems so, based on my local test), then it's quite possible
stable folks will figure out themselves they need 3/4 as a stable-dep-off:
4/4 but won't backport 2/4, unless you act quickly enough on their mails.

---

The way I see if often happen is that Greg sends a mail that it bounced and
soon after Sasha replies with fixed up version/series. Which could very
easily be 3/4 + 4/4 without 2/4.

reviews.author: Vlastimil (SUSE)
reviews.summary:
Gave Reviewed-by

Reviewer Vlastimil noted that a premature capture cannot jeopardize full-pageblock clearing because compaction_capture() disallows capturing <pageblock_order pages from MOVABLE pageblocks for !MOVABLE allocations, and it's unlikely the capture will ever succeed in these scenarios.
sentiment_signals: unlikely the capture will ever succeed
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, a premature capture can't jeopardize the full-pageblock clearing
because compaction_capture() disallows capturing <pageblock_order page from
MOVABLE pageblock for !MOVABLE allocations.

It's thus unlikely the capture will ever succeed in these scenarios. But
it's not wrong.

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton expressed skepticism about the patch, asking 'What's that' without providing any specific technical details or concerns.
reviews.raw_body:
What's that.

hp2:/usr/src/25> grep -ri stable-dep ../gitlog Documentation  
hp2:/usr/src/25>

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Andrew Morton expressed frustration with the patch series and requested that the Fixes tag be included in all four patches, but did not provide any technical feedback or suggestions for improvement.
reviews.raw_body:
My brain hurts.  I'll include

Fixes: e3aa7df331bc ("mm: page_alloc: defrag_mode")
Cc: <stable@vger.kernel.org>

in all four patches.

reviews.author: Greg Kroah-Hartman
reviews.summary: Reviewer Greg Kroah-Hartman expressed appreciation for the patch's explicit tagging, noting that it avoids potential issues and ensures a smooth implementation.
reviews.raw_body:
Thank you!  Don't make us "figure it out" as that way lies struggles and
time delays and the huge chance I get it wrong.  Tagging them all
ensures none of this happens and it all "just works".

thanks,

greg k-h
